title: pilosella × cinerea
intro: Pilosella × cinerea, also known as Cinereous Mouse-ear Hawkweed, is a rare, hybrid plant species that results from the cross-pollination of Pilosella cinerea and Pilosella officinarum. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits associated with Pilosella × cinerea include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable addition to herbal medicine. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
